Goal is to reduce ambiguity and make mis-parses visible immediately. Acceptance criteria: (1) CLI add output includes parsed date/time when present, (2) TUI add flow shows parsed date/time in status/inline confirmation, (3) behavior is non-blocking and does not require confirmation to save in SLC v1, (4) docs include examples and mention timezone/reference-date assumptions.

CLI add now prints parsed_when when created item has when_date, TUI add status now shows parsed when date/time, and both capture paths parse using local reference date (Local::now().date_naive()). Added docs/examples for output plus timezone/reference-date assumptions and tests for CLI/TUI confirmation helpers. Verified with cargo test -p agenda-cli -p agenda-tui and cargo clippy -p agenda-cli -p agenda-tui -- -D warnings.

title: Hashtag capture: implement unknown-hashtag behavior and UX
description: Implement the policy chosen in bd-3qv for unknown hashtags in item text. Scope should include capture-time behavior, user-visible feedback (creation confirmation or warning), and regression tests. Acceptance: #High/#Follow-up still map to existing categories without creating #prefixed categories; unknown hashtag behavior matches policy; docs updated.

title: T017: Implement rule engine in engine.rs
description:
Implement rule engine in agenda-core/src/engine.rs: process_item() — depth-first walk of category hierarchy, evaluate conditions, fire actions (Assign + Remove). Takes a Store reference and a Classifier.

The engine evaluates two levels of auto-assignment:
1. ImplicitString (name-based, zero-config): check category.enable_implicit_string flag. If true, run Classifier against item text and category name. The bool flag is the source of truth — do NOT check the conditions vec for Condition::ImplicitString.
2. Profile (assignment-based, user-configured): check the conditions vec for Profile { criteria } entries. Evaluate as set-membership: item must be in ALL criteria.include categories AND NOT in ANY criteria.exclude categories. Ignore virtual_include/virtual_exclude/text_search (those are for View queries, Phase 4). Profile conditions create cascading rules — e.g. 'if in Urgent AND Project Alpha, assign to Escalated.'

On match: assign with source AutoMatch, fire actions. Assign actions add targets. Remove actions are collected but deferred (not applied mid-cascade). Existing assignments are skipped (sticky — engine never revokes).

See spec/prompts/t017-rule-engine.md for full implementer prompt. See spec/design-decisions.md §1-3 for design rationale.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Claimed 2026-02-15 13:20. Plan: implement process_item single-pass rule engine with implicit/profile matching, assign/remove action handling, and focused unit tests.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. Implemented single-pass process_item in engine.rs with depth-first hierarchy walk, implicit string + profile condition matching, sticky assignment semantics, action handling (Assign immediate, Remove deferred), and 8 focused engine tests.

title: T019: Implement subsumption in engine
description: Implement subsumption in engine: when assigning to child, create implicit assignments for all ancestors (source: Subsumption). Walk up the parent chain from the assigned category and create Assignment records with source=Subsumption for each ancestor. These ancestor assignments should not re-trigger condition evaluation (they are bookkeeping).

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Claimed 2026-02-15 16:28. Plan: implement subsumption ancestor assignment hook in engine assign path; add tests for multi-level, stickiness, action non-firing, and no extra fixed-point churn.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. Added subsumption to engine assignment flow in engine.rs: successful AutoMatch/Action assignments now create sticky ancestor assignments with source=Subsumption and origin=subsumption:child-name; existing assignments are preserved; subsumption does not fire ancestor actions and is excluded from pass new_assignments. Added tests for basic and multi-level subsumption, no-overwrite, no ancestor action firing, non-counting in pass result, and action-triggered subsumption.

title: T027: Implement edit-through logic
description: Implement edit-through operations: insert_in_section assigns on_insert_assign + view.criteria.include. remove_from_section unassigns on_remove_unassign. remove_from_view unassigns remove_from_view_unassign. These are the assignment side-effects that make Views editable — the user drags/inserts items and category assignments change implicitly. Each operation should trigger process_item for cascading.

Acceptance criteria: (1) define parser policy modes (at minimum strict_next_week vs inclusive_next), (2) default is documented, (3) tests pinned to reference date 2026-02-16 covering this/next Tuesday behavior per mode, (4) behavior is deterministic and documented for all weekdays.

title: T020: Implement mutual exclusion in engine
description: Implement mutual exclusion in engine: when assigning to child of exclusive parent, unassign from sibling children. If a category's parent has is_exclusive=true, assigning an item to that category must first unassign the item from all other children of the same parent. Only one child of an exclusive parent can hold an item at a time.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Claimed 2026-02-15 16:41. Plan: enforce parent-based mutual exclusion before assignment persistence for AutoMatch and Action paths; unassign sibling children immediately; keep subsumption ordering intact; add engine.rs tests for exclusive/non-exclusive and source-agnostic behavior.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. Implemented immediate mutual exclusion in engine assignment flow: before assigning a child, if its parent is exclusive, sibling child assignments are unassigned immediately from store and in-memory assignment state. Applied to both AutoMatch and Action assignment paths while preserving subsumption ordering. Added tests for basic exclusive switching, non-exclusive coexistence, engine-match exclusivity, action-path exclusivity, manual-source removal, and three-child sibling behavior.

title: T018: Implement fixed-point loop in engine
description:
Implement fixed-point loop in engine: wrap T017's single-pass logic in a loop that re-runs while new assignments are produced, with max 10 passes.

Key behaviors:
1. Loop re-runs hierarchy walk until a pass produces zero new assignments (fixed point).
2. Pass cap: if pass 10 still produces new assignments, return an error — this indicates a rule configuration bug.
3. Cycle detection: HashSet<(ItemId, CategoryId)> seen-set persists across passes. Already-seen pairs are skipped.
4. Deferred removals: Action::Remove targets accumulate during cascade, applied after loop exits. Removals do NOT trigger re-evaluation.
5. Remove regardless of source: Remove actions apply uniformly — Manual, AutoMatch, Action, Subsumption assignments are all removed (spec/design-decisions.md §4).
6. Atomicity (optional): if Store API allows, wrap in DB transaction for rollback on error. Skip if non-trivial.

See spec/prompts/t018-fixed-point-loop.md for full implementer prompt. See spec/design-decisions.md §3-5 for design rationale.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Claimed 2026-02-15 15:10. Plan: implement fixed-point wrapper around T017 pass logic with seen-pair cycle guard, deferred remove application after convergence, 10-pass hard error, optional transaction if low-scope.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. Implemented fixed-point process_item loop with up to 10 passes, persistent (ItemId, CategoryId) seen-set across passes, deferred Remove application after convergence, remove-regardless-of-source behavior, and transactional rollback on cap-exceeded errors. Added comprehensive engine tests for cascades, cycle convergence, cap error, deferred removes, and idempotent re-run.

title: T024: Implement Query evaluator in query.rs
description: Implement the Query evaluator: given a Query and a list of items, return items matching all criteria. Include = item must be assigned to ALL listed categories. Exclude = item must NOT be assigned to ANY. virtual_include/virtual_exclude = WhenBucket filter (uses T023). text_search = case-insensitive substring match on item text + note. All criteria are ANDed together.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Implementer prompt written: spec/prompts/t024-query-evaluator.md. Key points: evaluate Query against items slice with all 5 criteria ANDed. Empty fields are permissive. Takes reference_date for WhenBucket resolution. Returns matching items in input order.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Claimed 2026-02-15 17:50. Plan: implement pure query evaluator in query.rs with reusable item predicate; keep AND semantics explicit (including virtual bucket behavior), preserve input order, avoid DB coupling, and add focused tests for all criteria interactions and edge cases.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. Implemented evaluate_query(&Query, &[Item], reference_date) in query.rs with pure, deterministic filtering and preserved input order. All five criteria are ANDed (include/exclude, virtual include/exclude via resolve_when_bucket, text search across text+note). Added comprehensive tests for each criterion, mixed-criteria behavior, permissive empties, and virtual-include multi-bucket semantics. Also fixed T023 precedence so Tomorrow always wins over ThisWeek (including Saturday->Sunday).

title: T016: TUI smoke test script for end-to-end daily loop
description: Add a TUI smoke script covering add/move/remove/done/delete/edit/category flows.

title: T011: TUI category structural edits (rename/reparent/exclusive/implicit)
description: Add TUI structural category editing flows: rename, reparent, toggle exclusivity, and toggle implicit-string behavior with validation.

title: Exclusive categories: manual assign allows multiple sibling assignments
description:
When a parent category is exclusive (example: Priority with children High/Medium/Low), manual assignment through CLI can leave multiple siblings assigned to the same item.

Repro (from spec/literate-cli-demo-priority-and-assignment.md):
1. Create Priority with --exclusive, then children High, Medium, Low.
2. Create an item and run:
   - agenda category assign <item-id> High
   - agenda category assign <item-id> Medium
3. List item assignments.

Observed:
- Item has both High and Medium.

Expected:
- Assigning Medium should remove High (single-child invariant under exclusive parent).

Notes:
- This appears to affect manual assignment path specifically (category assign -> assign_item_manual).
- Track fix in domain layer so behavior is consistent across CLI/TUI/API paths.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. Manual assign now enforces exclusive sibling cleanup in agenda-core (Agenda::assign_item_manual) so assigning one child under an exclusive parent unassigns siblings. Added regression tests in crates/agenda-core/src/agenda.rs and recorded CLI validation in spec/literate-cli-demo-exclusive-fix-validation.md. Implemented on branch codex/s60-exclusive-manual-assign-fix, merged to codex/slc-v1 at commit dfcea30.

title: CLI UX: category create duplicate-name should guide assign existing category
description:
Users attempting to scope categories by branch may run category create for names like Priority/High and hit DuplicateName. This is correct domain behavior (global unique category names), but current UX does not guide next action.

Need: improve CLI create error messaging (or add helper command) so duplicate-name attempts clearly direct users to assign existing category instead of creating a duplicate.

Acceptance:
1) Duplicate create failure tells user category exists globally and suggests category assign <item-id> <name>
2) Message should be actionable and non-ambiguous
3) Demo/docs updated to show intended workflow (create once, assign many).

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. CLI category create now surfaces actionable duplicate-name guidance: explains global category uniqueness, includes existing category UUID, preserves requested parent context, and suggests agenda category assign <item-id> <name> for reuse. Added CLI unit test in crates/agenda-cli/src/main.rs and end-to-end validation demo in spec/literate-cli-demo-global-priority-reuse.md (global Priority reused across Project X/Project Y). Implemented on codex/tjz-category-create-ux, merged to codex/slc-v1 at d2e66d7.

title: T028: Define DateParser trait in agenda-core/src/dates.rs
description: Define the date parsing interface in agenda-core/src/dates.rs. Add DateParser trait with parse(&self, text: &str, reference_date: NaiveDate) -> Option<ParsedDate>. Introduce ParsedDate model type used by parser outputs and downstream engine wiring. Keep this task limited to interface + data shape (no parsing logic yet). Add focused unit tests that assert API behavior for no-match and successful return scaffolding.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done 2026-02-16. Added DateParser trait and ParsedDate contract in crates/agenda-core/src/dates.rs, including explicit span semantics and contract-level unit tests for no-match, success shape, reference_date behavior, and span round-trip. Merged to main via merge commit 89db997.

title: T016: Implement SubstringClassifier in matcher.rs
description: Implement SubstringClassifier in agenda-core/src/matcher.rs: case-insensitive word-boundary match, returns Some(1.0) or None. Respects enable_implicit_string flag. Should handle word boundaries properly (matching 'Sarah' in 'Call Sarah tomorrow' but not 'Sarahville').

title: T006: Create agenda-core/src/lib.rs with module stubs
description: Create crates/agenda-core/src/lib.rs with module stubs: model, store, engine, matcher, dates, query, undo

title: T026: Implement show_children section expansion in query.rs
description: Implement show_children expansion: when a section has show_children=true and its criteria is a single category include, auto-generate subsections for each direct child of that category. One level only, using the category's stored child order. Items matching parent but no child go to an unmatched subsection.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Implementer prompt written: spec/prompts/t026-show-children-expansion.md. Key points: expand sections with show_children=true and single-include criteria into subsections per child category. One level only. Unmatched subsection for items in parent but no child. resolve_view needs category hierarchy access — extend signature.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Claimed 2026-02-16 06:01 UTC. Plan: extend resolve_view to expand show_children sections using category child order, add per-subsection effective insert/remove assignment metadata, keep expanded parent items empty, and add one-level expansion tests including parent-name contextual unmatched labels.

comments.author: Matt S.
comments.text: Done. Extended resolve_view in query.rs to support show_children expansion for simple single-include section criteria only (design decision §16). Added ordered child subsections plus contextual parent-name unmatched subsection (e.g., Projects (Other)); expanded parent section is now container-only with items in subsections. Added subsection edit-through metadata (effective on_insert_assign and on_remove_unassign), updated resolve_view to accept category hierarchy input, and added tests for order, no-expansion cases, one-level behavior, empty children, multi-child membership, and metadata propagation.
